text.heading: ECOWAS Heads of State and Government Converge in Abuja
text.p: Nigerian President Umaru Musa Yar'Adua has said the transformation of the Executive Secretariat of the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS) into a Commission has engendered great challenges and greater opportunities towards the achievement of total integration in Africa.

Yar'Adua made this statement in his welcome address at the 34th Ordinary Session of the Authority of ECOWAS Heads of State and Government currently holding in Abuja, adding he was highly delighted to host the ECOWAS leaders in the Nigerian capital city.

According to him, the last 18 months have witnessed a relative socio-political and economic stability within the West African region as well as a general improvement in the security situation as there at present is no "active conflict" in the region.

He expressed his optimism that there will be greater commitment to the ideals of democracy and good governance, resulting not only in the consolidation of stability across the region but also the facilitation of requisite harmony of diverse domestic policies to accelerate the region's integration process.
He therefore called for a self appraisal on the progress of ECOWAS so far and consider the areas which require more attention in the efforts towards integration.

While various integration Protocols ; particularly the ones on Common External Tariff (CET), Free Movement of Goods and Persons, Rights of Residence and of Establishment as well as the ECOWAS Trade Liberalisation Scheme (ETLS), have had marked impact on efforts to promoting each West African country's economy, President Yar'Adua stated that non-tariff barriers to trade in the region still exists.
This according to him has arisen from conflicting interpretations of the Protocols in different countries in dissonance with the spirit and the letter of the protocols.

Therefore, leaders of West African nations should make it a duty to make the operation of the protocols more robust and efficient as only then can the peoples, development partners and international community be won to partner with ECOWAS in the realisation of its vision to transform the region into a major player in the global economy.
